conditions: Chronic low back pain (lumbar strain with intermittent sciatica)
medications: Hydroxyzine 25 mg as needed for acute anxiety
Naproxen 500 mg as needed for back pain (with food)
Cyclobenzaprine 5 mg at night as needed for muscle spasms
Melatonin 3 mg at bedtime as needed
health.mental_health: Stress and rumination have increased during the divorce and custody dispute. She has panic symptoms before court-related meetings, low motivation on non-custody days, and sleep disruption; she is in weekly therapy and working on coping plans for custody exchanges.
recent_visits: Physical therapy evaluation for low back pain (WakeMed outpatient PT) - 3 weeks ago
Telehealth psychiatry medication follow-up - 1 month ago
Primary care visit for insomnia and headaches - 2 months ago

finance.debt: Car loan (~$9k remaining) and credit card balance that rose during separation (~$6k). She has paid a $4,000 retainer to a family-law attorney and is tracking shared expenses while disputing reimbursement for child-related costs.
recent_transactions: Monthly physical therapy copays (3 visits, $35 each)

relationships: Estranged spouse: Daniel Zhang (contentious communication; custody dispute)
Child: 7-year-old daughter, Lila (primary focus; coordination around school and exchanges)
Best friend: Aisha Patel (confidante; helps with pickups during hearings)
Older brother: Javier Morales (out-of-state; supportive but worried about legal costs)
Neighbor: Erin McClain (informal support network; occasional childcare swaps)
social.social_media_activity: Keeps accounts mostly private and rarely posts; she avoids sharing anything about the divorce or her daughter’s schedule. She has muted/blocked several mutual friends and keeps screenshots of harassing messages for her attorney.
private_conversations: Allegations about the spouse’s alcohol use and reliability during parenting time
Discussions with her attorney about documenting exchanges and potential protective orders
Therapy-related disclosures about panic symptoms and intrusive thoughts
Concerns about the child repeating adult conversations at school
Plans for a custody evaluator interview and what to share

schedule.work_hours: Typically 8:30am–5:30pm Monday–Friday, with occasional early-morning field sampling and 1–2 overnight trips per quarter depending on project needs.
regular_appointments: Co-parenting coordination call (every other Sunday evening, per temporary order)
School pickup/after-school program coordination on custody days
upcoming_events: Custody mediation session (downtown Raleigh) - next Wednesday 9:00am
Pediatrician well-visit for Lila - in 3 weeks
Field sampling at Neuse River site - Friday 6:30am–11:00am
Court hearing for temporary custody/visitation modification - next month
schedule.daily_routine: Morning stretching/heat pack for her back, quick breakfast, commute to the office or to field sites. Midday she checks school messages and legal emails. Evenings are split between homework/dinner on custody days and paperwork, therapy exercises, and preparing exhibits for legal meetings on non-custody days.

professional.company: Mid-sized environmental consulting firm based in the Research Triangle that supports state and municipal infrastructure projects
projects: Surface water sampling and nutrient analysis for a Neuse River watershed monitoring contract
Environmental compliance audits for construction stormwater permits (NCDEQ coordination)
Phase I Environmental Site Assessment support for commercial redevelopment sites in Wake County
GIS-based mapping of potential contamination sources near municipal wellfields
professional.performance_issues: She has had a few late deliverables and has requested reduced travel during active custody proceedings. Her manager is supportive but has asked for clearer advance notice when court dates and mediation affect availability.
workplace_relationships: Direct manager: Kevin Hollis (supportive; helps adjust field schedules)
Field tech lead: Brianna Chen (covers early sampling when Marisol has custody exchanges)
Client contact at a municipal utilities department (professional but time-sensitive expectations)

hobbies: Trail walking at Umstead State Park
Yoga (gentle/restorative classes when her back allows)
Cooking simple weeknight meals with her daughter
Reading climate and environmental policy books
habits: Uses a heating pad and does PT exercises most nights
Keeps a detailed custody/exchange log and saves receipts for child-related expenses
Limits caffeine after noon to reduce anxiety
Occasional stress drinking on non-custody nights (1–2 glasses of wine, trying to cut back)
Avoids dating publicly and keeps her location services off during exchanges
lifestyle.living_situation: Renting a two-bedroom apartment in Raleigh after moving out of the marital home; she is trying to keep the child’s school district stable while the divorce is pending and the housing situation is part of the custody negotiations.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly Mediterranean-leaning (fish, vegetables, whole grains), tries to reduce inflammatory foods for back pain; keeps quick kid-friendly staples and limits sugary snacks at home.
